brief_justification: Paper A focuses on the Stieltjes constants, their integral representations, and their role in the Laurent expansion of the Hurwitz and Riemann zeta functions, particularly in relation to the Li criterion for the Riemann hypothesis. Paper B, in contrast, derives identities involving the Riemann zeta function, binomial coefficients, and harmonic numbers using elementary analysis. There is no shared underlying machinery or role-for-role correspondence between the methods or mathematical structures in the two papers.
